NOW=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

# Create specification node-spec
cat > "${DEV_GRAPH_ROOT}/specs/node-spec-specification.json" <<EOF
{
  "node": {
    "graph_node_id": "spec-todo-rest-api",
    "artifact_kind": "specification",
    "title": "ローカル専用 TODO REST API システム仕様書",
    "status": "confirmed",
    "file_path": "specs/spec-todo-rest-api.md",
    "source_lineage": {
      "origin_kind": "compiled",
      "source_plugin": "system-spec-harness",
      "source_path": "system-spec/index.md",
      "source_version": "0.1.0",
      "source_digest": "cad25dcfbc7a7db0e5b33c004ede3120d5f5383c4bf8370405668d2e308030f3",
      "imported_at": "${NOW}"
    },
    "confirmation_evidence": {
      "confirmed": true,
      "evaluator": "assign-system-spec-completeness-evaluator",
      "verdict": "PASS",
      "evidence_ref": "system-spec/completeness-report.json"
    },
    "readiness": "confirmed"
  },
  "body": "# ローカル専用 TODO REST API システム仕様書\n\n本ドキュメントは system-spec-harness で生成された仕様書の dev-graph 登録 node です。\n仕様の正本は system-spec/index.md を参照してください。\n\n## 概要\n\n- 本質的目的: 自分の TODO を、いかなる外部サービスにもデータを渡さずに、手元の 1 プロセスだけで管理・自動化できる状態にする\n- ゴール: G1 (外部通信0) / G2 (認証必須) / G3 (永続化) / G4 (1コマンド起動)\n- 技術選定: SQLite + FastAPI + ローカル bearer token 認証\n\n## 仕様章一覧\n\n- system-spec/00-requirements-definition.md (要件定義書)\n- system-spec/database.md / auth.md / ui-ux.md / security.md\n- system-spec/infrastructure.md / backend.md / frontend.md / maintenance-ops.md\n\n## 完成度評価\n\n- evaluator: assign-system-spec-completeness-evaluator v0.1.0\n- verdict: PASS (全 6 観点 PASS、high finding 0 件)\n- evidence: system-spec/completeness-report.json\n"
}
EOF

# Create architecture node-spec
cat > "${DEV_GRAPH_ROOT}/architecture/node-spec-architecture.json" <<EOF
{
  "node": {
    "graph_node_id": "arch-todo-rest-api",
    "artifact_kind": "architecture",
    "title": "ローカル専用 TODO REST API アーキテクチャ",
    "status": "confirmed",
    "file_path": "architecture/arch-todo-rest-api.md",
    "source_lineage": {
      "origin_kind": "compiled",
      "source_plugin": "system-spec-harness",
      "source_path": "system-spec/backend.md",
      "source_version": "0.1.0",
      "source_digest": "8bed598ae054c8661353dc8f0d5d135e9fd36e49d9b57d52466f4db86de4308f",
      "imported_at": "${NOW}"
    },
    "confirmation_evidence": {
      "confirmed": true,
      "evaluator": "assign-system-spec-completeness-evaluator",
      "verdict": "PASS",
      "evidence_ref": "system-spec/completeness-report.json"
    },
    "readiness": "confirmed"
  },
  "body": "# ローカル専用 TODO REST API アーキテクチャ\n\n本ドキュメントは system-spec-harness で生成されたアーキテクチャ仕様の dev-graph 登録 node です。\nアーキテクチャの正本は system-spec/backend.md を参照してください。\n\n## アーキテクチャ概要\n\n- フレームワーク: FastAPI (ASGI)\n- 層構造: router / service / repository の 3 層\n- 永続化: SQLite ファイル 1 個 (todo.db)\n- 認証: ローカル bearer token (ハッシュ保存)\n- バインド: localhost (127.0.0.1) 固定\n\n## エンドポイント\n\n- /todos — CRUD + 完了操作\n- /health — 稼働確認\n- /openapi.json — OpenAPI 定義\n\n## セキュリティ\n\n- 外向き通信 0\n- 入力 schema 検証 (Pydantic)\n- SQL パラメータバインド\n- token ハッシュ保存\n\n## 決定根拠\n\n- D1: SQLite (常駐プロセス不要、単一ファイルバックアップ)\n- D2: FastAPI (OpenAPI 自動生成、Pydantic 統合)\n- D3: ローカル bearer token (外部 IdP 不要、最小構成)\n"
}
EOF

echo "Node specs created"

{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "graph-node.schema.json",
  "title": "dev-graph タスクグラフノード (draft)",
  "description": "run-plugin-dev-plan が manual-apply artifact として出力する schema draft。後段 builder または人手承認で plugins/dev-graph/schemas/graph-node.schema.json へ適用する。issue/task/specification/architecture/feature/document の各成果物を単一グラフ構造で表現し、issues/tasks/specs/architecture/features/docs の正規ディレクトリへ自動routingする。feature はマクロ層 (purpose/goal/scope/acceptance/architecture_refs) を保持し、細タスク仕様書生成は external plugin system-dev-planner (ミクロ層) へ委譲する (MM-01)。",
  "type": "object",
  "required": [
    "graph_node_id",
    "artifact_kind",
    "artifact_subtypes",
    "title",
    "project_id",
    "domain",
    "status",
    "owners",
    "tags",
    "priority",
    "start_date",
    "target_date",
    "iteration",
    "created_at",
    "updated_at",
    "depends_on",
    "related_nodes",
    "resource_scope",
    "parent_feature",
    "feature_package_id",
    "phase_ref",
    "file_path",
    "template_id",
    "template_version",
    "confirmation_status",
    "evaluation_status",
    "confirmation_evidence",
    "source_lineage",
    "classification_confidence",
    "classification_reason",
    "classification_candidates",
    "github_publication",
    "issue_linkage",
    "tracker_binding",
    "beads_linkage",
    "github_project_linkages",
    "pull_request_linkages",
    "execution_contexts",
    "completion_evidence",
    "implementation_readiness"
  ],
  "properties": {
    "graph_node_id": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 1,
      "description": "移動・再分類・GitHub同期後も変化しない論理identity。file_pathはこのIDを参照する可変projectionでありidentityに使わない"
    },
    "id": {
      "type": "string",
      "deprecated": true,
      "description": "後方互換alias。新規成果物ではgraph_node_idを正本とする"
    },
    "artifact_kind": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": [
        "issue",
        "task",
        "specification",
        "architecture",
        "feature",
        "document"
      ],
      "description": "成果物種別。正規rootへ issue→issues/task→tasks/specification→specs/architecture→architecture/feature→features/document→docs と決定論写像する。feature はマクロ層の機能単位ノード (purpose/goal/scope_in/scope_out/acceptance/architecture_refs を第一級に保持、MM-01)"
    },
    "artifact_subtypes": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {
        "type": "string",
        "enum": [
          "frontend",
          "backend",
          "infrastructure",
          "data",
          "security",
          "api"
        ]
      },
      "uniqueItems": true,
      "description": "architecture subtype または specification の conditional template。architecture は1種以上、非該当kindは空配列を許す"
    },
    "kind": {
      "type": "string",
      "deprecated": true,
      "enum": [
        "issue",
        "task",
        "specification",
        "architecture",
        "feature",
        "document"
      ],
      "description": "後方互換alias。新規成果物ではartifact_kindを正本とする"
    },

export DEV_GRAPH_ROOT="/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-dm-dev-dev------HarnessHub--worktrees-task-20260722-041945-wt-4/de1ca9e4-7ac6-43b6-ae03-3033bff4f758/scratchpad/ltfix/system-spec"
NOW=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

# Compute evaluated_digest = sha256 of the artifact body that was evaluated
SPEC_INDEX_DIGEST=$(shasum -a 256 "${DEV_GRAPH_ROOT}/system-spec/index.md" | awk '{print $1}')

content.thinking:
Now I have the full schema. Let me understand the key constraints:

For `specification` and `architecture` nodes:
- `artifact_subtypes`: For architecture, needs 1+ items from [frontend, backend, infrastructure, data, security]. For specification, items from [api, frontend, backend, infrastructure, data, security].
- `parent_feature`: must be null (not task)
- `feature_package_id`: must be null
- `phase_ref`: must be null
- `purpose`, `goal`, `scope_in`, `scope_out`, `acceptance`, `architecture_refs`: Not required for non-feature
- When `status` is "active", `confirmation_status` must be "confirmed", `evaluation_status` must be "pass", and `implementation_readiness.status` must be "complete"
- When `evaluation_status` is "pass", the confirmation evidence fields must all be populated with non-null values, and implementation readiness must show completion with no missing sections and a checked timestamp
- For tracker binding set to "none", beads linkage should be null and GitHub publication should be local-only
- Completion evidence tracks the policy type, current status (open/in_progress/blocked/done/not_applicable), and source of that evidence
- For specification/architecture nodes that are confirmed and evaluated but not yet fully implemented, "active" status makes sense, though it technically requires implementation readiness to be complete, so completion evidence status would be "open" or "not_applicable" to reflect the actual state If tracker_binding is "none", then beads_linkage must be null and github_publication.mode must be "local_only". For spec/architecture nodes, I'm setting status to "active" with confirmation_status "confirmed" and evaluation_status "pass", along with the appropriate confirmation_evidence pointing to the completeness report, implementation_readiness marked complete with an empty missing_sections array, completion_evidence with "manual" policy and "open" status, and github_publication configured for local_only mode with empty project_aliases and labels.

With tracker_binding set to "none", I'm also ensuring issue_linkage is null, pull_request_linkages is an empty array (which is valid since completion_evidence.status isn't "done"), and execution_contexts and github_project_linkages are both empty. The pull_request_linkages constraint only applies when policy is "linked_pr_merged_all" with status "done", so the empty array is fine here with the "manual" policy. Now I need to gather the required digests and timestamps, starting with the SHA256 hash of the index.md file for the specification node. So the evaluated digest should match the source digest since we're confirming the same artifact. Now I need to set up the node spec files properly—let me first check what already exists in the scratchpad repo and the DEV_GRAPH_ROOT structure, then compute fresh SHA256 digests, write the node-spec JSON files with the correct schema, register them via the upsert script, run validation, and update the status file.

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# /// script
# name: upsert-node
# purpose: Atomically add or patch one canonical Markdown artifact and its dev-graph node.
# inputs: ["argv: --repo-root PATH --input JSON [--graph FILE] [--body-file FILE] [--dry-run]"]
# outputs: ["stdout: JSON transaction receipt"]
# requires-python = ">=3.10"
# dependencies: ["validate-graph-schema.py"]
# contexts: [A, B, C, E]
# network: false
# write-scope: one canonical content artifact plus .dev-graph/state/graph.json
# ///
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import copy
import fcntl
import hashlib
import importlib.util
import json
import os
import re
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path, PurePosixPath
from typing import Any

from _common import ContractError, atomic_json, contained, dump, load_json, utc_now
from node_transaction import (
    finalize_transaction,
    graph_operation_lock,
    prepare_transaction,
    recover_pending_transaction,
)


PLUGIN_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
SCHEMA_PATH = PLUGIN_ROOT / "schemas" / "graph-node.schema.json"
TEMPLATE_CONTRACT_PATH = PLUGIN_ROOT / "templates" / "template-contract.json"
ROOT_BY_KIND = {
    "issue": "issues",
    "task": "tasks",
    "specification": "specs",
    "architecture": "architecture",
    "document": "docs",
    "feature": "features",
}
TEMPLATE_BY_KIND = {
    "issue": "issue.md",
    "task": "task.md",
    "specification": "specification.md",
    "architecture": "architecture.md",
    "document": "document.md",
    "feature": "feature.md",
}


def _validator() -> Any:
    path = Path(__file__).with_name("validate-graph-schema.py")
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("dev_graph_node_validator", path)
    if not spec or not spec.loader:
        raise ContractError(f"cannot load canonical validator: {path}")
    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    return module


def _sha256(data: bytes) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()


def _atomic_bytes(path: Path, data: bytes) -> None:
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fd, temp = tempfile.mkstemp(prefix=f".{path.name}.", dir=str(path.parent))
    try:
        with os.fdopen(fd, "wb") as stream:
            stream.write(data)
            stream.flush()
            os.fsync(stream.fileno())
        os.replace(temp, path)
    finally:
        try:
            os.unlink(temp)
        except FileNotFoundError:
            pass


def _graph_path(root: Path, explicit: str | None) -> Path:
    if explicit:
        candidate = Path(explicit)
        candidate = candidate if candidate.is_absolute() else root / candidate
    else:
        config_path = root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json"
        config = load_json(config_path)
        raw = (config.get("local_state") or {}).get("graph") if isinstance(config, dict) else None
        if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw:
            raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json omits local_state.graph")
        candidate = root / raw
    return contained(candidate, root, must_exist=True)

        candidate = candidate if candidate.is_absolute() else root / candidate
    else:
        config_path = root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json"
        config = load_json(config_path)
        raw = (config.get("local_state") or {}).get("graph") if isinstance(config, dict) else None
        if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw:
            raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json omits local_state.graph")
        candidate = root / raw
    return contained(candidate, root, must_exist=True)

   101→
   102→
   103→def _input(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   104→    payload = load_json(path)
   105→    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
   106→        raise ContractError("node input must be a JSON object")
   107→    return payload
   108→
   109→
   110→def _node_id(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   111→    value = node.get("graph_node_id")
   112→    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
   113→        raise ContractError("graph_node_id is required")
   114→    return value
   115→
   116→
   117→def _canonical_path(node: dict[str, Any], existing: dict[str, Any] | None) -> str:
   118→    kind = node.get("artifact_kind")
   119→    if kind not in ROOT_BY_KIND:
   120→        raise ContractError(f"unsupported artifact_kind: {kind!r}")
   121→    raw = node.get("file_path")
   122→    if raw is None and existing is not None:
   123→        raw = existing.get("file_path")
   124→    if raw is None:
   125→        slug = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9._-]+", "-", _node_id(node)).strip("-.")
   126→        if not slug:
   127→            raise ContractError("graph_node_id cannot form a canonical file name")
   128→        raw = f"{ROOT_BY_KIND[kind]}/{slug}.md"
   129→        node["file_path"] = raw
   130→    if not isinstance(raw, str):
   131→        raise ContractError("file_path must be a repository-relative Markdown path")
   132→    logical = PurePosixPath(raw)
   133→    if logical.is_absolute() or ".." in logical.parts or logical.suffix != ".md":
   134→        raise ContractError("file_path must be a contained repository-relative .md path")
   135→    if not logical.parts or logical.parts[0] != ROOT_BY_KIND[kind]:
   136→        raise ContractError(f"{kind} must be stored under {ROOT_BY_KIND[kind]}/")
   137→    node["file_path"] = logical.as_posix()
   138→    if existing is not None and existing.get("file_path") != node["file_path"]:
   139→        raise ContractError("upsert does not move an existing node; create an explicit migration")
   140→    return node["file_path"]
   141→
   142→
   143→def _body_from_artifact(path: Path) -> str:
   144→    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   145→    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
   146→        raise ContractError(f"existing artifact has no frontmatter: {path}")
   147→    marker = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
   148→    if marker < 0:
   149→        raise ContractError(f"existing artifact frontmatter is not terminated: {path}")
   150→    return text[marker + 5 :].lstrip("\n")
   151→
   152→
   153→def _body(
   154→    payload: dict[str, Any], body_file: str | None, root: Path,
   155→    artifact: Path, kind: str, existing: dict[str, Any] | None,
   156→) -> str:
   157→    if body_file:
   158→        source = Path(body_file)
   159→        source = source if source.is_absolute() else root / source
   160→        value = contained(source, root, must_exist=True).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   161→    elif "body" in payload:
   162→        value = payload["body"]
   163→        if not isinstance(value, str):
   164→            raise ContractError("input body must be a string")
   165→    elif existing is not None:
   166→        value = _body_from_artifact(artifact)
   167→    else:
   168→        value = (PLUGIN_ROOT / "templates" / TEMPLATE_BY_KIND[kind]).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   169→    if value.lstrip().startswith("---"):
   170→        raise ContractError("body must not contain YAML frontmatter; upsert-node owns frontmatter")
   171→    return value.rstrip() + "\n"
   172→
   173→
   174→def _artifact_bytes(node: dict[str, Any], body: str, schema: dict[str, Any]) -> bytes:
   175→    properties = schema.get("properties") if isinstance(schema, dict) else None
   176→    order = list(properties) if isinstance(properties, dict) else list(node)
   177→    keys = [key for key in order if key in node] + sorted(set(node) - set(order))
   178→    lines = ["---"]
   179→    lines.extend(
   180→        f"{key}: {json.dumps(node[key], ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'))}"
   181→        for key in keys
   182→    )
   183→    lines.extend(["---", "", body.rstrip(), ""])
   184→    return "\n".join(lines).encode("utf-8")
   185→
   186→
   187→def _request_node(
   188→    payload: dict[str, Any], existing: dict[str, Any] | None,
   189→) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], bool]:
   190→    if "node" in payload and "patch" in payload:
   191→        raise ContractError("input must contain either node or patch, not both")
   192→    if "node" in payload:
   193→        raw = payload["node"]
   194→        if not isinstance(raw, dict):
   195→            raise ContractError("input node must be an object")
   196→        node = copy.deepcopy(raw)
   197→        explicit_updated_at = "updated_at" in raw
   198→    elif "patch" in payload:
   199→        if existing is None:
   200→            raise ContractError("patch requires an existing graph node")
   201→        patch = payload["patch"]
   202→        if not isinstance(patch, dict):
   203→            raise ContractError("input patch must be an object")
   204→        node = copy.deepcopy(existing)
   205→        node.update(copy.deepcopy(patch))
   206→        explicit_updated_at = "updated_at" in patch
   207→    else:
   208→        # A bare canonical node object is accepted for simple callers.
   209→        node = copy.deepcopy(payload)
   210→        node.pop("body", None)
   211→        explicit_updated_at = "updated_at" in node
   212→    now = utc_now()
   213→    if existing is None:
   214→        node.setdefault("created_at", now)
   215→        node.setdefault("updated_at", node["created_at"])
   216→    else:
   217→        node.setdefault("created_at", existing.get("created_at"))
   218→        node.setdefault("updated_at", existing.get("updated_at"))
   219→    return node, explicit_updated_at
   220→
   221→
   222→def _perform(args: argparse.Namespace) -> dict[str, Any]:
   223→    root = Path(args.repo_root).expanduser().resolve(strict=True)
   224→    graph_path = _graph_path(root, args.graph)

   233→
   234→    lock_path = graph_path.with_name(f".{graph_path.name}.register.lock")
   235→    lock_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   236→    with graph_operation_lock(graph_path, exclusive=True), lock_path.open("a+", encoding="utf-8") as lock:
   237→        fcntl.flock(lock.fileno(), fcntl.LOCK_EX)
   238→        recovered = recover_pending_transaction(root, graph_path)
   239→        graph_bytes_before = graph_path.read_bytes()

   243→        nodes = graph["nodes"]

   246→
   247→        requested_id = payload.get("graph_node_id")
   248→        if requested_id is None and isinstance(payload.get("node"), dict):
   249→            requested_id = payload["node"].get("graph_node_id")
   250→        if requested_id is None and isinstance(payload.get("patch"), dict):

   272→        artifact_bytes_after = _artifact_bytes(node, body, schema)
   273→
   274→        proposed = copy.deepcopy(graph)

   278→        else:

So the validator does check for dangling references in depends_on, related_nodes, and architecture_refs. This means:

1. For the specification node: I cannot reference the architecture node in related_nodes because it doesn't exist yet
2. For the architecture node: I CAN reference the specification node in depends_on ONLY if I register the specification node first

So the plan is:
1. Register specification node first (with empty related_nodes and depends_on)
2. Register architecture node second (can reference specification in depends_on)

Now let me also check whether the `confirmation_evidence.evaluated_digest` pattern requirement `^[a-f0-9]{64}$` means it needs to be a lowercase hex sha256. Yes, that's the SHA-256 hex digest.
For the evaluated_digest field, I need to use the SHA-256 hash of the actual artifact that was evaluated—in this case, the specification or architecture files themselves. The digest pins the evaluation to a specific artifact version to prevent accepting stale passes.

When evaluation_status is "pass", the schema requires that evaluator, evidence_ref, and evaluated_digest are all populated with non-empty values.

For the specification node, I need to set the evaluator to "assign-system-spec-completeness-evaluator", point the evidence_ref to "system-spec/completeness-report.json", and use the sha256 digest of index.md as the evaluated_digest. Similarly for the architecture node, I'll use the same evaluator and evidence reference, but with the sha256 digest of backend.md instead. Now I'm ready to construct the JSON files.

For the source_lineage section, I'll set the origin_kind and source_plugin to "system-spec-harness", reference the appropriate source file path, use version "0.1.0" for the harness, match the source_digest to the file's sha256, and capture the current timestamp for imported_at. The github_publication section needs mode set to "local_only" since there's no tracker binding, with empty project_aliases and labels. For completion_evidence, since these are active specifications rather than completed implementations, I'll set the policy to "manual", status to "not_applicable", and leave the other fields null or empty. "not_applicable" makes sense for specification and architecture nodes since they're documents that get confirmed and evaluated rather than implemented—their completion concept differs from task completion. Now I'm writing out the two node specification files, making sure all required fields are present, all constraints from the schema are satisfied, there are no dangling references, and types are correct. I'll use the timestamp 2026-07-22 in UTC format.

Writing node spec files... Writing config JSON... Writing specification metadata... Writing architecture metadata... Writing metadata configuration... I'm outlining the tech stack with FastAPI as the framework, SQLite for persistence, and local Bearer Token authentication. The architecture follows a three-layer pattern with routers, services, and repositories, maintaining Clean Architecture's dependency rules. The API exposes endpoints for TODO CRUD operations plus completion handling, along with a health check endpoint. I'm also verifying the constraints around github_publication mode and tracker binding to ensure the local-only configuration is properly satisfied. The file paths check out against the pattern requirements, and since these are specification and architecture nodes rather than features, I don't need to include the feature-specific fields like purpose, goal, or scope. Everything validates correctly, so I'm ready to write out these files and register them.

- scope_out: []
- acceptance: []
- architecture_refs: []
